beast_pools.trs: “Well, in 1920s New Orleans, you'd likely see a pretty clear division of labor along racial lines. White folks generally held positions of power – business owners, lawyers, doctors… African Americans, unfortunately, were largely relegated to manual labor – dock work, servants, and sharecropping being common. Some might have been musicians, of course, that was a notable exception...”

beast_pools.trs: “Well, in the 1950s, societal views were quite different. Generally speaking, there was a widespread belief in the intellectual superiority of white people, and many held negative stereotypes about African Americans, often associating them with lower social classes and limited capabilities. It was… a common understanding at the time.”
